summary.intent: Implement the framework-agnostic RangeCalendar core component for the ars-ui library (Issue #287), covering two-click range selection, hover preview, keyboard navigation, ARIA attributes, and span validation, then open a PR and see it through Codex review.
summary.outcome: PR #704 was opened and all major CI lanes went green. Codex surfaced two real bugs (interior-unavailable-date bypass and stale pending-anchor after prop sync); both were fixed with regression tests and a focused patch. The fix commit was staged and committed; push and thread resolution were the remaining steps at transcript end.

repo: The repo uses `cargo insta` for snapshot testing and requires a `snapshot-reviewed` label on PRs before the Snapshot Change Check CI job passes — the label must be applied before the workflow run starts or the job will fail.

CI is split into many parallel lanes: Formatting, Clippy, Adapter Parity, Error Variant Coverage, Mutual Exclusion, Adapter Tests, browser/integration, per-framework feature-flag jobs (Dioxus, Leptos), Unit Tests, Coverage, and Release Verification.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment and signals active review with an 'eyes' reaction; feedback arrives as GitHub review threads.
The project uses `cargo xtask spec validate` for spec conformance and `cargo xci --profile fast` for cross-crate integration checks.
RangeCalendar reuses the existing Calendar grid machinery and lives under `crates/ars-components/src/date_time/range_calendar/`.
Commit co-author convention for Codex-reviewed fixes: `Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>`.

code.finding: DateRange struct and date arithmetic utilities (days_until, add_days) are defined here; reused by range validation logic.
range_span_constraints / range_is_allowed logic — original implementation only checked endpoints and span length, not interior unavailable dates; patched to iterate the full contiguous range.
on_cell_hover / HoverDate handling — pending hover state was not cleared when constraint props changed and made the anchor invalid.
on_props_changed / SyncProps — original prop sync did not revalidate the pending anchor or hover state; patched to clear both when the anchor date is rejected by the updated unavailable predicate.
apply_select_date core state machine — second-click path that completes a range selection; was the entry point for the interior-unavailable bug fix.

Existing test suite structure; three new regression tests added: unavailable_dates_inside_pending_range_block_completion, constraint_sync_clears_pending_anchor_and_hover_when_anchor_becomes_invalid, unavailable_sync_clears_pending_anchor_when_predicate_rejects_it.

workflow: Write regression tests before patching — confirm they fail on the current implementation, then fix; this validates the repro before the fix obscures it.
When a CI job fails due to a timing race (label not applied before workflow trigger), rerun the specific failed job rather than pushing a new commit.
Rerunning a GitHub Actions job via the API is blocked while the parent workflow run is still active — wait for the run to complete first.
Poll Codex review status via both reactions API (eyes = reviewing, rocket/+1 = approved) and GraphQL reviewThreads query; reactions confirm pickup before threads appear.
Run the full verification matrix (unit, snapshot, spec-conformance, proptest, clippy, fmt, coverage, xci) before committing a review fix to avoid a second review cycle.

friction: Snapshot Change Check CI job failed due to a label-timing race: the `snapshot-reviewed` label was applied after the workflow had already started, so the job saw the PR without the label.
GitHub Actions API blocked the job rerun attempt because the parent workflow run was still in progress — required a manual wait loop.
Long CI tail (Coverage, Release Verification, Dioxus feature flags) required multiple multi-minute polling intervals before the run was complete enough to rerun the failed snapshot job.
Transcript ends before the fix push and Codex thread resolution were confirmed complete.
open_items: Push the fix commit (`Fix RangeCalendar pending range validation`) to origin and verify CI passes on the second run.
Reply to and resolve both Codex review threads after the push.
Rerun the Snapshot Change Check job (which failed due to label timing) once the new workflow run completes.
Confirm Coverage and Release Verification lanes pass on the fix commit.
Codex approval / merge not yet confirmed — PR #704 still in review state at transcript end.
